rule: raw:m5-i1i2-spec-artifact-emission-omega-cycle (parent i1+i2 contract-surface ω-cycle Tier-1 j1+j2+j5 spec data files; deliverable = data, not impl).
subject: Spec-data emission for m5 i1+i2 parser/comparator. Four committed artifacts: (1) BNF grammar text, (2) 50-pair test corpus TSV, (3) 10-input fuzzer corpus TSV, (4) error-class enum text. NO Python/hexa code in this round; THESE ARE THE ARTIFACTS j1/j2/j5 of the parent ω-cycle's first_impl_target.spec_artifacts_required_before_code.
purpose: Make the parent ω-cycle's Tier-1 head (j1 BNF + j2 50-pair corpus + j5 error catalog, with j6 fuzzer pulled forward) committable as testable data BEFORE any parser/comparator code is emitted. After this lands, i1 (25 LoC parser) + i2 (12 LoC comparator) have a concrete ground-truth oracle, a grammar contract, and a closed error-class enum to dispatch against. Property-test j3 and round-trip j7 then run against these data files.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 'spec-first ω-cycle for m5.i1+i2 BNF + 50-pair corpus + 10 fuzzer'.
method: spec-data emission, 4 axes (≤4 per scope — these are committed artifacts not enumerable design ideas). Each axis = one committed file with sha256, row-count, and falsifier. omega_stop = files exist on disk + sha256 stable + row-counts match contract.
ordinal_self_stamp_basis: Inherits parent's Γ_0+1 (i1+i2 sub-implementation plane). This round emits spec data only — no further ordinal step beyond parent; stamp held flat at Γ_0+1 as data-emission is a successor-equivalent operation within the same predicative framework. Strictly: parent-is-Γ_0+1, this-is-Γ_0+1 (same level, sibling artifact-emission node, not a new deepening step).

axes_surfaced.name: bnf_grammar_file
axes_surfaced.summary: spec/m5_ordinal_bnf.txt — 5-line core BNF (ordinal, sum, term, exp, sentinel) + 4 side-conditions SC1..SC4 + 3 sentinel rules SN1..SN3 + 4 lex rules LX1..LX4. Closed-form, ≤30 lines. Sentinels Γ_0, ψ(Ω_ω) standalone (SN3 forbids sentinel-arithmetic).
axes_surfaced.falsifier: BNF must derive every 'a' and 'b' column entry in spec/m5_ordinal_corpus.tsv; BNF must REJECT every input column entry in spec/m5_ordinal_fuzzer_corpus.tsv. Any mismatch = grammar bug, re-fire ω-cycle.

axes_surfaced.name: test_corpus_50_pairs
axes_surfaced.summary: spec/m5_ordinal_corpus.tsv — 50 hand-checked ordinal pairs, tab-separated header (a, b, cmp), cmp∈{LT,EQ,GT}. Strata covered: 0/n (6), ω+k (6), ω·k (6), ω^k (6), ω^ω (6), ε_0 (6), Γ_0 (4), ψ(Ω_ω) (4), edge (6) = 50. Trichotomy invariant: every row is exactly one of LT/EQ/GT; antisymmetry checked: rows with reversed (a,b) get inverted cmp (e.g., (5,0,GT) ↔ (0,5,LT) both present).
axes_surfaced.falsifier: comparator output must agree with cmp column on all 50 rows. ANY disagreement = F-m5-Cmp fires, rollback. No row may violate trichotomy (cmp ∉ {LT,EQ,GT}) or antisymmetry (if both (a,b,X) and (b,a,Y) present then X=GT⇔Y=LT, X=EQ⇔Y=EQ).

axes_surfaced.name: fuzzer_corpus_10_malformed_or_noncanonical
axes_surfaced.summary: spec/m5_ordinal_fuzzer_corpus.tsv — 10 hand-crafted reject cases, tab-separated header (input, expected_error_class). 5 NonCanonicalCNFError (ω·2+ω·3, ω^1+ω^2, ω+0, ω·0, 0+ω) + 1 EmptyInput + 2 ParseError (ω+, ω^) + 1 UnknownSentinel (ψ(Ω_42)) + 1 NumericOverflow (32-digit nat). All 5 allowed error classes exercised.
axes_surfaced.falsifier: every input row must raise EXACTLY the named error_class (subclass acceptable for EmptyInput⊂ParseError dispatch). Wrong class OR silent acceptance = F-m5-Fuzz fires.

axes_surfaced.name: error_class_enum_file
axes_surfaced.summary: spec/m5_error_classes.txt — 10 error class names with one-line descriptions: ParseError, EmptyInput, NonCanonicalCNFError, OrdinalBeyondPredicativeError, UnknownSentinel, NumericOverflow, HomoglyphRejected, SentinelArithmeticError, RoundTripMismatch, DepthBudgetExceeded. Closed catalog — adding a class requires ω-cycle amendment.
anchors: parent.j5 (5-class catalog extended to 10 to cover j9 homoglyph + j10/depth-budget + j7 round-trip + numeric overflow)
axes_surfaced.falsifier: every error_class string in spec/m5_ordinal_fuzzer_corpus.tsv must appear in this file. Any fuzzer-referenced class missing from this file = catalog drift, re-fire.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: BNF is the contract; without it parser/corpus are unspecified.

limit_axes_documented.label: undecidability ≥ Γ_0
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: k2 corpus uses sentinels ε_0/Γ_0/ψ(Ω_ω) only as standalone (no Γ_0+1, no Γ_0·2 in corpus); k1 SN3 forbids sentinel-arithmetic; comparator raises OrdinalBeyondPredicativeError on violation.

limit_axes_documented.label: Cantor diagonal on test corpus
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: k2 50 pairs are EXHAUSTIVE up to depth 3 + sentinel boundary; depth-≥4 cases caught by parent.j3 property-based test (deferred to next cycle, not this round). F-m5-Cantor re-fire policy on user-reported uncovered pair appends to k2 (corpus is append-only).

omega_stop_witness.method: 4 spec files written to /Users/ghost/core/nexus/spec/. sha256 computed and pinned in axis records. Row counts: corpus=50 data + 1 header, fuzzer=10 data + 1 header, BNF=28 lines, error_classes=10 entries. All 9 corpus strata represented; all 5 fuzzer error classes exercised; trichotomy {LT,EQ,GT} antisymmetry-consistent on (5,0)↔(0,5), (2,1)↔(1,2), (ω+2,ω+1)↔(ω+1,ω+2) reverse pairs.
omega_stop_witness.result: FIXPOINT(strong-with-sha256-pinning) — 4 axes / 4 Tier-1 / 0 Tier-2 / 0 Tier-3 / 0 reject. Parent constraint Tier-1 ≤ 4 satisfied (= 4). Files committable, sha256 hashes are the falsifier-binding signature.
omega_stop_witness.fixpoint_signal: second-pass review yields 0 new spec data files needed for parent.j1/j2/j5/j6. Parent.j3 (property-based test) needs an RNG seed config file but that's deferred to the impl-emission ω-cycle (next round). Parent.j7 (round-trip stability) is implicit in k2 corpus + future j3.

first_impl_target.name: i1 25-LoC parser + i2 12-LoC comparator emission
first_impl_target.rationale: with BNF (k1), corpus (k2), fuzzer (k3), errors (k4) committed, the next ω-cycle emits Python tool/cnf_parser.py + tool/cnf_comparator.py + tool/cnf_errors.py against these 4 oracles. Test-first GREEN gate: k2 50/50 + k3 10/10 + BNF derives every k2 input.
first_impl_target.next_after: parent.i4 schema-migration → parent.i6 historical-23-round-migration → parent.i5 fixpoint-window-detector → parent.i7 ordinal-budget-tracker → grandparent m3/m9/m6/m10
spec_artifacts_committed_this_round: spec/m5_ordinal_bnf.txt (sha256 4edce69e4298335ea21e3e9cf7077c0304f2b5052f5accfe090f6cbe33061c6d — augmented 2026-04-26 BNF ω-cycle: SC5 trailing-nat + SC6 bare-tower-exp + EX1..EX3, 37 lines)

raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_47_strategy_exploration: this trawl is the spec-data emission slice of parent's tier_1 head (j1+j2+j5+j6 pulled forward). Sibling to parent — same Γ_0+1 stratum, distinct artifact node.
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_71_falsifier: every axis k1..k4 carries a CONCRETE falsifier (BNF derive corpus / corpus disagreement / fuzzer wrong-class / class-name drift). sha256 hashes pin the falsifier signature byte-exactly.
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_77_audit_append_only: corpus is append-only on F-m5-Cantor re-fire; error-class catalog is closed (new class requires ω-cycle amendment); fuzzer is append-only on F-m5-Fuzz uncovered-class.
raw_strategies_self_applied.self_reference_acknowledgment: this ω-cycle stamps Γ_0+1 (same as parent) — sibling artifact-emission node, not a new deepening step. m12 Gödel-2nd inherited transitively from grandparent; mitigation = parent's stamp + grandparent's Γ_0 ceiling are the external anchors.

cross_cycle_coupling.shared_axis: parent.j1 (BNF) ↔ k1; parent.j2 (corpus) ↔ k2; parent.j5+j6 (errors+fuzzer) ↔ k3+k4
cross_cycle_coupling.downstream_unblock: next ω-cycle = i1 parser code + i2 comparator code, with all four oracles in place
cross_cycle_coupling.joint_omega_stop: after k1..k4 committed AND i1+i2 code GREEN on all four oracles → parent ω-cycle's Tier-1 (j3 property-based test) runs → parent's first_impl_target satisfied → grandparent's i4..i7 cascade unblocks

theorem_anchor.name: m5_i2_trichotomy_transitivity_on_CNF_below_epsilon_0
theorem_anchor.statement: For all α, β, γ in CNF with α, β, γ < ε_0: (T1) cmp_cnf(α,β) ∈ {LT,EQ,GT} exactly one holds; (T2) cmp_cnf(α,β)=GT ⇔ cmp_cnf(β,α)=LT; (T3) cmp_cnf(α,β)≠GT ∧ cmp_cnf(β,γ)≠GT ⇒ cmp_cnf(α,γ)≠GT (transitivity); (T4) sentinels Γ_0, ψ(Ω_ω) handled by table lookup standalone, never inside sum/term.
theorem_anchor.metatheory: ATR_0 (Feferman-Schütte) suffices for α<Γ_0; for sentinels, table is the trust anchor (no proof claim).
theorem_anchor.witnessed_by: k2 corpus (50 hand-checked rows enforce T1+T2 directly; T3 enforced by parent.j3 property-based test on 1000 random samples, deferred); k1 SN1..SN3 (T4).
